name: Vienna Game
description: 2.Nc3 keeping f4 in reserve — the Vienna Gambit and quiet lines.
source: curated repertoire (scripts/repertoires/vienna.pgn) + lichess explorer, ratings 1200/1400/1600, speeds blitz/rapid

children.comment: En passant, before ...f5 fixes the centre in Black's favour.
The recapture opens the b-file and builds a big centre with d4.
The right answer - everything else favours White quickly.
The classic lift: Qh4, f5 and Ng5 aim at the king.
With f4 played the c5 bishop stares at g1: castling is illegal until it is gone. Trade it off first.
Re-aim at f7; Black's dark squares are already loose.
The famous problem move: g7 is attacked and 4...Qf6?? 5.Nd5! wins material.
The pin is annoying now that ...Be7 costs a tempo.
A pleasant Italian where f4 is still coming as a plan.

variations.name: Gambit main line
variations.intro: 2.Nc3 then 3.f4: a King's Gambit where Black cannot safely accept. The main line 3...d5 is Black's best - know it well from both sides.

variations.name: vs 2...Nc6
variations.intro: Without ...Nf6 there is no ...d5 counter: develop with Bc4, delay f4 until it comes with full force.

variations.name: vs 2...Bc5
variations.intro: 2...Bc5 ignores the centre. Take it with d4 at the right moment and develop with tempo on the exposed bishop.
